recommendations.author: George R.R. Martin
recommendations.blurb: The series opener that defined modern grimdark political fantasy — seven POVs and a willingness to break hero conventions that reset reader expectations (Ending 8.7), with Westeros rendered in extraordinary depth (Originality 9.4, Insights 9.2). Closest stylistic analogs in your library are Erikson and Abercrombie, though Martin's intricate plotting is more grounded in political realism than either. Confidence caveat: Prose (7.8) is highest-risk — reviewers consistently call Martin's writing 'competent/workmanlike' rather than stylistically distinguished, which could drag the predicted WA below 8.48.
